chemicals.chemical_formula: Not provided in the given sources.
chemicals.concentration_notes: Pennyroyal is distinctly noted as being "highly toxic" compared to other, milder garden mints47. Additionally, the risk is heavily dependent on concentration; extracted essential oils are far more potent and dangerous than the raw plant. Highly concentrated oils can cause severe, life-threatening toxicity through ingestion, inhalation (such as from a diffuser), or even direct absorption through th
chemicals.description: in cats: Cats lack the specific liver enzymes required to properly break down and metabolize these plant essential oils23. Because the cat's body cannot process them, the compounds accumulate and cause systemic poisoning. While many mints mainly cause gastrointestinal irritation, the compounds in Pennyroyal are exceptionally severe and attack the liver, leading to liver damage, liver failure, seiz
chemicals.name: Essential oils. The sources specifically identify pulegone and menthol as the toxic essential oil compounds found within the mint family

description: Pennyroyal (Mentha pulegium) is a mint family herb that is severely toxic to cats — significantly more dangerous than common mint or peppermint. Its primary toxic compound is pulegone, a monoterpene ketone that is metabolised in the liver to the reactive compound menthofuran. This metabolite causes severe hepatotoxicity and can be lethal, especially in the concentrated essential oil form historically used as a flea repellent.
emergencyNote: Gastrointestinal Decontamination (Emesis induction, Gastric Lavage, and Activated Charcoal)
name: Pennyroyal
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: pulegone (primary); metabolised to menthofuran in the liver — the true hepatotoxic agent
Mechanism: menthofuran causes direct hepatocellular necrosis (liver cell death); glutathione depletion amplifies the damage
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy; severe cases cause acute liver failure with jaundice, tremors, seizures, and collapse
Pennyroyal essential oil is especially dangerous — do NOT use it as a flea treatment; even topical application can cause fatal liver failure
Any exposure to pennyroyal oil is a veterinary emergency; plant ingestion also warrants prompt vet evaluation

symptoms.name: Liver damage progressing to liver failure
symptoms.notes: Unlike common garden mints (like spearmint or peppermint) that primarily irritate the digestive tract, Pennyroyal is explicitly noted for its severe systemic toxicity that attacks the cat's liver, which can ultimately lead to death1.... 2. Seizures and Neurological Impairment

symptoms.name: Seizures, tremors, uncoordinated gait, weakness, and lethargy
symptoms.notes: Seizures and profound neurological depression are hallmarks of severe mint/essential oil toxicity and are specifically associated with highly toxic mint plants like Pennyroyal25. 3. Gastrointestinal Distress

symptoms.name: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive drooling, loss of appetite, and difficulty swallowing
symptoms.notes: The essential oils in the mint family are highly irritating to a cat's gastrointestinal tract36. With Pennyroyal, these symptoms may occur alongside or precede more severe systemic organ failure23. 4. Respiratory Issues

symptoms.name: Difficulty breathing, coughing, and sneezing
symptoms.notes: In severe cases, the ingestion or inhalation of the essential oils found within toxic mint plants can cause significant respiratory distress45. Note: The provided sources do not specify the exact hourly onset times for these symptoms. If you suspect your cat has been exposed to Pennyroyal, immediate

treatments.description: To prevent the highly toxic essential oils and plant material from being fully digested and absorbed into the bloodstream, the veterinarian will remove the stomach contents. This is achieved by medically inducing vomiting or, in extreme cases, performing a gastric lavage (stomach pump)12. Following this, the vet may administer activated charcoal to bind to and neutralize any remaining toxins in th
treatments.name: Gastrointestinal Decontamination (Emesis induction, Gastric Lavage, and Activated Charcoal)
treatments.notes: You should never attempt to induce vomiting at home (such as by giving the cat hydrogen peroxide or salt). There are no safe over-the-counter products for inducing vomiting in cats, and doing it yourself can cause significant injury, aspiration, or worsen the poisoning23. This procedure must be done

treatments.description: The veterinarian will administer IV fluids to rehydrate the cat (especially if they have suffered from excessive vomiting and diarrhea), stabilize their system, and help support the body's organs as they process the toxins
treatments.name: Intravenous (IV) Fluid Therapy and Hospitalization
treatments.notes: Unlike common garden mints that may only cause mild stomach upset, Pennyroyal is highly toxic and is specifically known to cause severe, potentially fatal liver damage and failure4.... Due to the severe nature of Pennyroyal poisoning, the cat will require hospitalization alongside the fluid therapy

treatments.description: The vet will administer specific supportive medications to manage the cat's clinical symptoms. This typically includes anti-nausea injections to stop active vomiting and antacids or gastric protectants to shield and soothe the irritated stomach lining
treatments.name: Anti-nausea Injections and Gastric Protectants (Antacids)
treatments.notes: These medications are crucial for keeping the cat as comfortable as possible while they weather the episode of illness and gastrointestinal distress caused by the mint's volatile oils1.
